name: Kalmyk
notes: Kalmyk Oirat (хальмг келн), the Mongolic language of Kalmykia, written since 1938 in a Cyrillic alphabet: the Russian letters plus Әә /æ/, Өө /ø/, Үү /y/, Җҗ /d͡ʒ/, Ңң /ŋ/ and Һһ /ɣ/. Kalmyk has front–back vowel harmony and a phonemic vowel-length contrast; the Cyrillic orthography denotes long vowels by DOUBLING the vowel letter, so the doubled digraphs (аа әә ээ ии оо өө уу үү) are given as their own keys and win under maximal-munch matching. Most consonant letters also have palatalised realisations before front vowels / ⟨ь⟩; only the plain values are mapped here, so palatalisation is under-specified rather than invented. The soft sign ⟨ь⟩ and hard sign ⟨ъ⟩ are NOT mapped: they denote palatalisation of the preceding consonant, not a segment of their own. Ф, п, щ and the iotated letters are essentially confined to Russian loans. The historic Clear Script (Todo bichig) is out of scope. INPUT CONTRACT: Cyrillic Kalmyk text, Unicode NFC.
